element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player chooses from 8 location-based teams or 2 all-star teams before match begins
Select starting pitcher, batting order, manage 8 starters + 4 bench players per team
Starting pitchers available all game; relievers have low stamina requiring strategic substitution
Hitters vary in solid contact ability and power; demonstrated immediately through early at-bats
Player speed variation becomes apparent when advancing bases; Vince Coleman notably fastest
Speed-dependent mechanic; difficult to execute with slow runners, trivial with Coleman
Steer ball left/right during flight; effectiveness varies by pitcher (Ryan/Clemens fast, Valenzuela deceptive movement)
Pitcher stamina decreases over innings; ball speed and steerability degrade, requiring relief pitcher substitution
Non-player-controlled; occurs automatically with no documented individual player variation
Fielding errors advance runners and credit hitter with hit; creates momentum shifts
Substitutable for any position mid-game; allows flexibility in roster management
Game ends immediately if one team ahead by 10+ runs after any completed inning

typical_relief_triggers: Strikeout ends batter without scoring risk
typical_tension_triggers: Runners on base with less than two outs
Approaching mercy rule threshold (9+ run deficit)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Inning-based cycle rhythm; tension builds through baserunner accumulation and pitcher fatigue; relief occurs via outs and defensive plays. Each full game contains 9 innings of tension/release cycles with variable duration per at-bat.

difficulty_curve.early_game: Accessible baseline; player learns hitting and pitching mechanics during first inning at-bats
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Difficulty escalates through pitcher fatigue mechanics requiring strategic relief pitcher management; opponent adaptation increases
difficulty_curve.late_game: Cumulative stamina effects force substitutions; closer margins heighten tension as mercy rule threshold approaches
notable_spikes: First at-bat against high-speed pitcher (Ryan, Clemens)
Pitcher fatigue point (typically innings 5-7) forces suboptimal relief pitcher use
Opponent mercy rule window (trailing by 8-10 runs after inning 5+)

notable_level_patterns.description: Game divided into 9 innings; each inning contains potential for multiple at-bats (tension cycles) followed by side-out (relief). Inning-based pacing creates predictable rhythm with variable execution time per batter.

Starting pitcher enters at full stamina; declining stamina across innings reduces ball speed and steerability. Forces player to strategically deploy relievers (with low stamina) before starter becomes ineffective.
10+ run differential after completed inning triggers immediate game end; creates hard constraint on comeback potential. Shapes late-game urgency and decision-making.

Significant variation in player attributes (Vince Coleman speed, Nolan Ryan fastball, Fernando Valenzuela breaking ball movement) creates asymmetric matchup scenarios. Same roster selection yields different difficulty profiles.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Any matchup leveraging speed differential or pitcher specialty

sources_used: wikipedia: R.B.I. Baseball - Gameplay section, History section
